message:
test: comprehensive expansion of network fault simulation assertions

network_fault_simulation.rs:
  Every existing test in this file has been strengthened with additional
  invariant checks beyond the original single-assertion pattern:

  - partition_blocks_spawn_delivery: now verifies spawn not received,
    not accepted, not completed; running count == 0; no ResultDelivery
    sent. Helper assert_no_task_progress extracted.
  - partition_heal_allows_delivery: verifies first-task stays dropped
    after heal; second-task is received AND completes; running drains.
  - duplication_handled_by_idempotency: verifies single completion,
    running count drains, at least one ResultDelivery emitted.
  - network_packet_duplication: verifies payload bytes preserved,
    zero drops, zero corruption in dup-only mode.
  - reordering_preserves_delivery: verifies correct completion count,
    running drains to zero.
  - cancel_dropped_by_partition: (already strong from prior commit)
  - crash_restart_lifecycle: strengthened with explicit spawn-after-
    restart completion assertion.

  New helper: count_sent(harness, from, to, msg_type) counts messages
  of a specific type in the harness trace. has_spawn_accepted helper
  added for SpawnAccepted event matching.

  New tests:
  - delayed_delivery_under_latency: verifies tasks complete despite
    configurable network latency, with acceptance and completion checks.
  - partition_during_active_task_preserves_worker_state: spawns a task,
    partitions while running, verifies task keeps running on the worker.
  - byzantine_fault_with_payload_corruption: verifies corrupted spawn
    is rejected, clean spawn succeeds, corruption metric incremented.
  - asymmetric_partition_allows_one_way_delivery: verifies A->B blocked
    but B->A works, with result delivery confirmation.
  - simultaneous_bidirectional_spawn_under_latency: two-way spawn under
    100ms latency, verifies both tasks complete.
  - cascading_fault_partition_then_corruption: partition phase blocks
    delivery, corruption phase allows but corrupts, verifies both
    metrics and no false completions.

t6_deterministic_integration_fault_injection.rs:
  Strengthened epoch-boundary and fault-injection test assertions with
  additional state machine invariant checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
